SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr

Interface Accumulator

public interface Accumulator
Tracks aggregation state for a single group during aggregate computation.

An accumulator is created per group by AggregateUDF.createAccumulator(). DataFusion calls updateBatch(java.util.List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector>) with input data, then evaluate() for the final result.

For distributed/partitioned execution, DataFusion uses state() and mergeBatch(java.util.List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector>) to combine partial results from multiple partitions.

Example: 

class SumAccumulator implements Accumulator {
    private long sum = 0;

    public void updateBatch(List<FieldVector> values) {
        BigIntVector col = (BigIntVector) values.get(0);
        for (int i = 0; i < col.getValueCount(); i++) {
            if (!col.isNull(i)) sum += col.get(i);
        }
    }

    public ScalarValue evaluate() { return new ScalarValue.Int64(sum); }
    public List<ScalarValue> state() { return List.of(new ScalarValue.Int64(sum)); }

    public void mergeBatch(List<FieldVector> states) {
        BigIntVector partialSums = (BigIntVector) states.get(0);
        for (int i = 0; i < partialSums.getValueCount(); i++) {
            if (!partialSums.isNull(i)) sum += partialSums.get(i);
        }
    }

    public long size() { return Long.BYTES; }
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: Accumulator

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ScalarValue
    evaluate()
    Returns the final aggregate result as a scalar value.
    void
    mergeBatch(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector> states)
    Merges intermediate state from other accumulators into this one.
    long
    size()
    Returns the estimated memory size of this accumulator in bytes.
    List<ScalarValue>
    state()
    Returns the intermediate accumulation state as scalar values.
    void
    updateBatch(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector> values)
    Updates the accumulator state with a batch of input values.

  • Method Details

    • updateBatch

      void updateBatch(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector> values)
      Updates the accumulator state with a batch of input values.

      Each element in values is a column vector for one argument of the aggregate function. For a single-argument aggregate like SUM(x), values has one element.

      See Accumulator for an example.

      Parameters:
      values - the input column vectors, one per argument
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: Accumulator::update_batch

    • evaluate

      ScalarValue evaluate()
      Returns the final aggregate result as a scalar value.

      See Accumulator for an example.

      Returns:
      the final aggregate result
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: Accumulator::evaluate

    • state

      List<ScalarValue> state()
      Returns the intermediate accumulation state as scalar values.

      Used for distributed execution: partial accumulators serialize their state via this method, then another accumulator merges them via mergeBatch(java.util.List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector>). The number and types of values must match AggregateUDF.stateFields(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field).

      See Accumulator for an example.

      Returns:
      the intermediate state values
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: Accumulator::state

    • mergeBatch

      void mergeBatch(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector> states)
      Merges intermediate state from other accumulators into this one.

      Each element in states is a column vector containing partial state values from multiple accumulators. The vectors correspond to the fields returned by AggregateUDF.stateFields(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field).

      See Accumulator for an example.

      Parameters:
      states - the partial state column vectors to merge
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: Accumulator::merge_batch

    • size

      long size()
      Returns the estimated memory size of this accumulator in bytes.

      Used by DataFusion for memory management. Include the size of all internal state.

      See Accumulator for an example.

      Returns:
      the estimated memory size in bytes
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: Accumulator::size
